headline: Article 49A's expulsion carve-out, blocked from court by the Societies Act
context: Article 49A(2)(c) of the Federal Constitution exempts expulsion from triggering loss of an MP's seat. Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 bars courts from hearing party-membership disputes. The two produce a constitutional protection courts cannot practically interpret.

cards.text: A Labuan MP's appeal over the cessation of his Bersatu membership was dismissed at the Court of Appeal on 29 April 2026.
cards.sub: Article 49A(2)(c) of the Federal Constitution exempts expulsion from triggering loss of MP status. After 3 years 7 months, no court has interpreted the carve-out on its merits.
sources: The Edge Malaysia, 29 April 2026, 'Suhaili fails in appeal to reinstate suit over cessation of Bersatu membership' (Stage 3 verified)
The Star, 29 April 2026, 'Labuan MP fails in appeal to revive lawsuit over Bersatu membership cessation' (Stage 3 verified)
Federal Constitution Article 49A; Societies Act 1966 Section 18C (Stage 2 omission flag, Stage 3 case-disposition source)

cards.h: Article 49A inserted by Constitution (Amendment) (No. 3) Act 2022 (Act A1663), in force 5 October 2022.
cards.s: The text prevents a party from causing an MP's seat to be vacated merely by expelling that MP.
sources: Laws of Malaysia, Constitution (Amendment) (No. 3) Act 2022, Act A1663, Gazette 6 September 2022 (Stage 3 verified)
Bernama, 4 October 2022, anti-party hopping law enforcement notice (Stage 3 verified)

cards.h: Zuraida Kamaruddin's case (CoA December 2024) was decided on Contracts Act s.75 proportionality, not Article 49A.
cards.s: The court held the RM10 million bond disproportionate and reduced compensation to RM100,000. The breach was upheld. PKR's Federal Court appeal hears 16 July 2026.
sources: Court of Appeal judgment text, Saifuddin Nasution Ismail v Zuraida Kamaruddin, JE51/2024, 20 December 2024 (Stage 3 verified primary source)
The Edge Malaysia, 10 April 2026, Federal Court fixes 16 July 2026 hearing (Stage 3 verified)

cards.h: Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi (May 2026) used Article 51 to vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa before leaving PKR.
cards.s: Resign seat first, leave party second. The Election Commission treated the vacancies as Article 51 resignations and stated no by-elections under Article 54(1)'s two-year window.
sources: Bernama, 24 May 2026, EC statement on Pandan and Setiawangsa vacancies (Stage 3 verified)

cards.text: Article 49A(2)(c) names a constitutional protection. Section 18C of the Societies Act tells the courts not to reach it. Two laws, one shielded carve-out.
sources: Societies Act 1966 Section 18C; Suhaili Court of Appeal dismissal on Section 18C grounds, 29 April 2026 (Stage 2 omission flag + Stage 3 source attribution)

cards.h: A locked door with no available key
cards.text: Article 49A(2)(c) is the constitutional protection. Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 keeps the courts from reaching the disputes that could test it.
Article 49A(2)(c) is a constitutional protection Section 18C makes unreachable. Until the two laws align or a case finds a procedural path, the carve-out's scope stays speculative.
sources: Suhaili Court of Appeal disposition on Section 18C grounds, 29 April 2026

Federal Constitution Article 49A (Act A1663, 2022); Societies Act 1966 Section 18C; Court of Appeal judgment text Saifuddin Nasution Ismail v Zuraida Kamaruddin JE51/2024 (Stage 3 primary); Suhaili Abdul Rahman Court of Appeal disposition 29 April 2026 (Edge Malaysia, The Star, Stage 3 verified); Bernama 24 May 2026 EC statement on Article 51 vacancies; T4A issue 1993
confidence: Medium-High. Constitutional and statutory text are gazette-verifiable. Zuraida Court of Appeal judgment text is a Stage 3 primary source. Suhaili disposition on Section 18C grounds verified by two independent Stage 3 news sources (Edge Malaysia, The Star, both 29 April 2026). Federal Court Zuraida appeal pending July 2026 is verified by Stage 3 (Edge Malaysia 10 April 2026). The 'no public reported judgment' formulation is now appropriately bounded — softened from the original 'zero substantive interpretations' absolute claim per Stage 3's UNVERIFIED status on the absence claim.
revisions: CORRECTED (Stage 3): Act A1664 → Act A1663 (correct gazette number per Laws of Malaysia)
CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'Four years of operative law' → '3 years 7 months' (October 2022 to May 2026)

CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'ruling that representatives cannot contract away their constitutional right to choose a party' → 'held PKR's RM10 million anti-defection bond disproportionate and reduced compensation to RM100,000. The breach was upheld.' Per primary source Court of Appeal judgment JE51/2024: the ruling was on Contracts Act s.75 proportionality, not constitutional rights.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 bias): 'Bersatu's expulsion' → 'the cessation of his Bersatu membership' across hook and headline. The 'expulsion' framing prejudges the constitutional question Section 49A(2)(c) addresses and adopts one side's legal characterisation.

REPHRASED (Stage 2 bias): 'Anti-hopping protection sidestepped without judicial test' → 'EC treated the vacancies as Article 51 resignations and stated no by-elections under Article 54(1)'s two-year window'. The original framing implied evasion; the corrected framing reports the legal mechanism the EC actually applied.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): 'No reported judgment interprets it on its merits' → 'no public reported judgment interprets the carve-out on its merits'. Softens the absolute absence claim to one bounded by this review.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): 'voter-protective' → 'prevents a party from causing an MP's seat to be vacated merely by expelling that MP'. Drops the intent claim that requires Hansard backing; retains the textual effect.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 as the structural reason the carve-out is untested. Verified via: (a) Stage 2 omission flag identifying Section 18C as the jurisdictional roadblock; (b) Stage 3 source attribution that the Suhaili Court of Appeal disposition turned on Section 18C grounds (The Edge Malaysia 29 April 2026; The Star 29 April 2026, both Stage 3 primary news sources).
INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Federal Court Zuraida appeal pending 16 July 2026. Verified via Stage 3 source (The Edge Malaysia 10 April 2026, https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/799391).
INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): EC no-by-election statement under Article 54(1)'s two-year window for Rafizi-Nik Nazmi. Verified via Stage 3 source (Bernama 24 May 2026).

STRUCTURAL REFRAME (Stage 6, drawing on Stage 2 + Stage 3): The original argument was 'carve-out untested' (a weak absence-claim). The synthesis upgrades to 'carve-out untested because Section 18C blocks courts from reaching it' — a structural mechanism finding. The headline, context, hook, reframe, analogy, and view cards all reflect this upgraded framing.

introduced_claims.claim: Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 ousts court jurisdiction over party-internal disputes including membership cessation.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 2 omission flag identifying Section 18C as the jurisdictional roadblock for party-membership disputes; Stage 3 source attribution that the Suhaili Court of Appeal disposition turned on Section 18C grounds (The Edge Malaysia 29 April 2026, The Star 29 April 2026).

introduced_claims.claim: PKR's appeal to reinstate the RM10 million Zuraida bond is fixed for Federal Court hearing on 16 July 2026.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 3 verified — The Edge Malaysia 10 April 2026, https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/799391

introduced_claims.claim: Election Commission stated no by-elections will be held for the Pandan and Setiawangsa vacancies because they fall within Article 54(1)'s two-year window before scheduled dissolution.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 3 verified — Bernama 24 May 2026, EC statement, https://bernama.com/en/politics/news.php?id=2559680

synthesis_notes: Two genuinely substantial corrections from Stage 2 and Stage 3 — both adopted in full, neither over-integrated. Stage 2's terminology flag ('expulsion' vs 'cessation') is the more politically loaded of the two findings and was applied across the whole document because the legal distinction is operative for the case outcome (the carve-out protects expelled MPs but Bersatu's argument turned on cessation, not expulsion). Stage 3's correction to the Zuraida holding was even more decisive — the original Stage 1 wording asserted a constitutional ruling that does not exist in the judgment. The corrected wording (proportionality under Contracts Act s.75; breach upheld) is grounded in Stage 3's primary source (judgment text JE51/2024) and is materially different in editorial implication: the Zuraida case is not the voter-protective precedent the original framing suggested. Both critiques were factually right; integration was straightforward. The structural addition (Section 18C as jurisdictional roadblock) emerged from Stage 2's omission flag and Stage 3's case-disposition source. This INTRODUCED claim is the most consequential addition in the synthesis — it turns a relatively weak absence-claim story into a structural-blockage story. Verified by two independent Stage 3 news sources on the Suhaili disposition (Edge Malaysia and The Star, both 29 April 2026), plus Stage 2's independent identification of Section 18C as a Societies Act jurisdictional bar. The synthesis is materially stronger and editorially safer than Stage 1. All length budgets honored after a tightening pass. No reframe template drift (avoided 'X is not Y, it is Z' pattern). No em-dashes in reader-facing fields. No scaffolding markers. Engine Stage 6 cross-check (sim-stage6-check.mjs --scenario ge15-actual) ran and raised one [MEDIUM] disagreement flag (synthesis does not mention Court Cluster / BN legal-exposure dynamics). This is a false positive for this issue — the keyword-based check triggered on PKR / Court of Appeal mentions in the cards, but the synthesis is about constitutional doctrine on MP-party-court jurisdiction, not coalition arithmetic. The engine has no structural reading on Article 49A or Section 18C; its coalition-arithmetic output is contextual background only, captured by the engine context block already in the brief.
